paper_title: Pollen Analysis and Insect Pollen Use Study

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze pollen data, focusing on pollen composition, diversity, and specialization among insect species across different sampling periods and regions.

steps.description: Data were always standardized to proportions (i.e. relative contributions with total 100), and analyses were performed using pollen types percentages.
steps.searchable_summary: standardize data to proportions and calculate pollen type percentages

steps.description: Mean pollen type richness and mean Simpson diversity were compared for the four insect species with Kruskal-Wallis and Dunn tests.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Kruskal-Wallis and Dunn tests on pollen type richness and Simpson diversity

steps.description: Complementary specialization d′ was calculated for each species for each sampling period using the R package 'bipartite'.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate complementary specialization d′ using bipartite package

steps.description: Mean d′ along the season was compared for the four species with Student t test.
steps.searchable_summary: compare mean d′ using Student t test

steps.description: H 2 ′ index was used for comparing the different networks across the season.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate and compare H 2 ′ index for network specialization

steps.description: Used GLMMs with a binomial error distribution to test the impacts of fixed factors on the proportion of pollen from woody plants.
steps.searchable_summary: fit GLMMs to test fixed factors on woody pollen proportion

steps.description: Predictors' significance was tested with Wald chi-square tests.
steps.searchable_summary: test predictors' significance using Wald chi-square tests

steps.description: Used similar GLMMs to test the proportion of pollen from non-agricultural plants.
steps.searchable_summary: fit GLMMs for non-agricultural pollen proportion

steps.description: Calculated landscape metrics using ArcGIS based on 2016 land-cover maps.
Confirmed no remaining spatial autocorrelation in the models by checking residuals against spatial coordinates with correlogram plots.
steps.searchable_summary: check for spatial autocorrelation using correlogram plots

methods.description: Procedures for processing insect samples to extract and analyze pollen grains, including chemical treatments and microscopic identification.

steps.description: Elytra and/or wings of sampled C. carnea and H. axyridis individuals were removed, and insects were thoroughly rinsed with ETOH.
steps.searchable_summary: remove insect parts and rinse with ethanol

steps.description: Insects were crushed and acetolysis was performed following Jones (2012).
All pollen samples were treated chemically with acetolysis and KOH, and mounted in glycerine following standard palynological methods.
steps.searchable_summary: treat pollen samples with acetolysis and KOH, mount in glycerine

steps.description: Pollen grains were identified under a light microscope based on palynological keys and a photo atlas.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen grains using microscope and reference materials

steps.description: For each sample, identified and counted up to 100 pollen grains whenever possible.
steps.searchable_summary: count and record pollen grains per sample
